summary: Backend testing completed for new email/password authentication system. All 22 tests passed covering signup, login, session validation, logout, and remember_me functionality. One CRITICAL security issue found: password_hash is exposed in /api/auth/me response.

critical.issue: SECURITY: password_hash field is returned in response. This exposes bcrypt hashed passwords to clients.
critical.fix: Exclude password_hash from User model response or create a separate UserResponse model without password_hash

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /api/auth/me to exclude password_hash from response - security vulnerability
critical_code_review_comments: SECURITY ISSUE: The User model includes password_hash field which is returned by /api/auth/me endpoint. Even though bcrypt hashes are computationally expensive to crack, exposing password hashes is a security anti-pattern.
Recommendation: Create a UserResponse model that excludes password_hash, or use model_dump(exclude={'password_hash'}) when returning user data

All auth endpoints working correctly: signup creates user with hashed password, login validates credentials and returns session token, remember_me controls session duration (30 days vs 1 day), logout invalidates session

test_credentials.note: Test users created and cleaned up automatically by pytest fixtures

seed_data_creation: No seed data required - tests create and cleanup their own test users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email/password auth system fully tested. All features working: signup (POST /api/auth/signup), login (POST /api/auth/login), session validation (GET /api/auth/me via X-Session-Token or Bearer token), logout (POST /api/auth/logout), remember_me flag (30 days vs 1 day session). CRITICAL: password_hash is exposed in /api/auth/me response - needs fix.

signup_tests.test_signup_success: PASSED - Creates user with hashed password, returns session token

login_tests.test_login_success: PASSED - Returns session token and user data
